headline: Storm hits Sunday — wrap outdoor tasks by Saturday
weather_notes: Sunday brings a 44% storm chance; Sunday night climbs to 74% rain/snow — Friday and Saturday are your only dry outdoor windows this week.
Any Bt applied Saturday has two dry days to activate before the storm; Sunday rain will wash off a fresh application.
Sunday night drops to 37°F, the coldest night this month — worth monitoring young tomato transplants (e1–e4) for chilling stress Monday morning.
Post-storm moisture Sunday–Monday removes any need to irrigate beds this weekend.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Four days overdue and Saturday is the last dry window before Sunday's rain washes off any fresh Bt application.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due Sunday but storms arrive that day — complete Saturday while conditions are dry.

suggested_tasks.title: Resow 2 missing bean spots in c1
suggested_tasks.description: Six of 8 beans are up and growing tall; resow the 2 gaps now before taller neighbors shade out late-sown seed.
